name: BizQuest Daily Alert System | Automate search to buy an established SMB

parameters.content:
## Personalized BizQuest Deals (Apify + Claude Haiku & Sonnet)

### How it works

1. Scrapes BizQuest via Apify daily at 5am (local timezone) using filters defined in your buy box.
2. Normalizes the listings and ranks them by SDE.
3. Loops one listing at a time, skipping any already in the master Google Sheet.
4. Scores each new listing 1–5 for buyer fit using Claude Haiku (1 is low, 5 is high).
5. Drafts a 3–5 sentence personalized message to the broker using Sonnet, only for listings that fit.
6. Appends every scored listing to the Sheet and posts the 4+ score listings to Slack with the outreach in a code block.

-> Basically, you get a shortlist of relevant deals with a copy/paste personalized broker message.

### Who this is for
1. Business buyers
2. Business owners
3. Investors
4. Entrepreneurs

### Setup steps

- [ ] Create a 16-column Google Sheet (cols: Title | Source | Keyword | Location | Asking Price | Revenue | SDE | SDE Estimated | Listed Date | Link | First Seen | Stage | Fit Rationale | Fit Score | Fit Confidence | Broker Message)
- [ ] In "Scrape BizQuest", replace the Apify task URL with your own link.
- [ ] In "Define Buy Box", edit all five fields — buyBox (free-text profile), keyword, cashFlowMin, priceMax, listingAgeDays. Every downstream node reads from here.
- [ ] Wire additional credentials: Anthropic, Google Sheets (OAuth2), Slack (OAuth).  
- [ ] Select your Google Sheet and your Slack DM/channel.
- [ ] Test with a manual run, confirm, then toggle Active.

The Apify scraper used in this workflow is here: https://apify.com/memo23/bizquest-scraper

## More at SMB Excel — smbexcel.com

## Schedule scrape and set buy box.

Runs the Apify scrape at the cron interval (default: 5am daily local timezone) Normalizes the returned listings into a common shape and imputes SDE at 10% of revenue when missing (flagged sdeEstimated:true downstream).

User MUST edit (a) Apify API url and (b) buy-box details

## Rank, loop, and dedupe

Ranks the day's batch by SDE and caps at the top 100. Loops one listing at a time and skips any already saved in the master google sheet — uses the canonicalized BizQuest listing URL as the unique id.

## Score buyer fit

Haiku 4.5 scores buyer fit 1–5 against your buy box text via a Structured Output Parser. Output: {rationale, score, confidence}. Every scored listing gets logged to the Sheet for audit. Only score 4 or 5 listings continue to Sonnet outreach + Slack alert.

## Draft broker outreach

Sonnet 4.6 drafts a 3–5 sentence broker outreach specific to the listing. Pulls the broker's first name from the listing when present, otherwise leaves "[Broker name]" as a placeholder for you to fill.

## Log and post to Slack

Appends winners to google sheet with broker message. Then posts everything you need to your Slack DMs.

## Log low-score listings

Score 1–3 listings are appended to the Sheet for audit review — no Slack ping, no Sonnet call (saves tokens; lets you spot scoring drift over time by reviewing the Sheet).

parameters.jsonBody: ={"filterKeyword": "{{ $('Define Buy Box').item.json.keyword }}", "filterCashFlowMin": {{ $('Define Buy Box').item.json.cashFlowMin }}, "filterPriceMax": {{ $('Define Buy Box').item.json.priceMax }}, "filterListingAgeDays": {{ $('Define Buy Box').item.json.listingAgeDays }}, "includeListingDetails": true, "maxItems": 1000, "proxy": {"useApifyProxy": true, "apifyProxyGroups": ["RESIDENTIAL"]}}

options.systemMessage:
=You are an acquisition analyst pre-screening business-for-sale listings for a buyer.

=== BUY BOX ===
{{ $('Define Buy Box').item.json.buyBox }}
=== END BUY BOX ===

Note: listings have already passed the buy-box filters at the scrape level. Your job is to assess business buyer FIT given the buy box above, not size.

=== YOUR TASK ===
Given a listing (with description body), output a JSON object with exactly three fields:
- rationale: ONE sentence, max 25 words, concrete, referencing a specific number or fact from the listing or description. No hedging. If SDE is flagged as estimated, mention it.
- score: integer 1–5 where 1 = poor fit, 3 = plausible, 5 = strong fit. When SDE is estimated, lean conservative — drop one point.
- confidence: 'low', 'med', or 'high'. Confidence reflects how sure you are in your own assessment (a confident rejection is valid). SDE estimated → rarely above 'med'.

Example output:
{"rationale":"Texas HVAC with $1.2M revenue, recurring commercial maintenance contracts mentioned in description, fits cleanly.","score":4,"confidence":"high"}

parameters.jsonSchemaExample: {"rationale":"Texas HVAC with $1.2M revenue, recurring commercial maintenance contracts, fits cleanly.","score":4,"confidence":"high"}

options.systemMessage:
=You are an acquisition analyst drafting an outreach message to a business broker on behalf of a self-funded searcher who is interested in a listed business.

=== BUY BOX ===
{{ $('Define Buy Box').item.json.buyBox }}
=== END BUY BOX ===

=== YOUR TASK ===
Draft a 3–5 sentence broker outreach message. The message must be:
- Conversational and direct, like a serious buyer (not breathless, not overly formal)
- Specific to THIS listing — reference at least one concrete fact, number, or phrase from the description that proves you read it
- MUST include one substantive question that demonstrates real engagement (about financials, customer mix, operations, transition, etc. — not a generic 'tell me more')
- Ends with '[Your name]' as a placeholder for the searcher's signature

Tone: treat the broker as a peer. Signal serious-buyer behavior through specificity, not enthusiasm.

=== OUTPUT RULES ===
- The 'brokerMessage' field in your output JSON must contain ONLY the message text — starting with 'Hi {broker first name},' if a Broker name is provided (e.g. 'Hi Kenneth,'), otherwise 'Hi [Broker name],' as placeholder
- End with '[Your name]'
- NO preamble, NO commentary, NO subject line, NO explanation before or after
- NO Markdown formatting, code blocks, headings, or quote blocks — plain text only, paste-ready
- 3–5 sentences total in the body of the message

parameters.jsonSchemaExample: {"brokerMessage":"Hi Kenneth, I came across the listing in Austin and the recurring commercial maintenance contracts caught my eye. Could you share the customer concentration? Thanks, [Your name]"}

assignments.name: buyBox
assignments.value:
==== BUY BOX ===

(CONFIGURE THIS — replace the example below with a few paragraphs about who you are and what you are looking for)

I am a [self-funded / sponsored / part-time] searcher based in [YOUR CITY, STATE], looking to acquire a [INDUSTRY] business. My background is [RELEVANT EXPERIENCE], and I have [RELEVANT OPERATING CONTEXT].

I am [full-time / part-time] on the search and plan to close within [TIMELINE]. Target deal size: [SDE RANGE], asking price [ASKING RANGE], [EBITDA MARGIN MINIMUM]+ margins, [RECURRING REVENUE / CUSTOMER MIX REQUIREMENTS], [YEARS IN BUSINESS MINIMUM]+ years in business, owner-operator transitioning out.

Verticals I want: [LIST INDUSTRIES YOU'D ACQUIRE IN].

Verticals I avoid: [LIST WHAT YOU WON'T BUY AND WHY].

Strategy: [HOLD AND OPERATE / ROLL-UP / FLIP].

Geography: [REGIONS YOU'LL OPERATE IN].

=== END BUY BOX ===
